New client onboarding & welcome guide

Welcome to Sugarcane Farm Manager

A simple, offline-first tool built specifically for sugarcane farmers — your fields, crop growth, workers, expenses, and weather, all in one place on your phone. No lost paper, no complicated math.

Why this app

Think of it as your digital farm notebook. It keeps track of everything you'd normally write down or try to remember — and does some of the thinking for you.

Works with zero signal
Every feature works out in the field, even with no internet. It saves to your phone first.
No complicated menus
Plain language throughout — no technical jargon to learn before you can use it.
Free cloud backup
Sign in once and your data backs up automatically. Skip it and it still works, saved just to your phone.
One tap to install
Add it to your home screen like any other app — no app store needed.

The Overview tab — your farm's summary, weather, calendar, and field recommendations

Quick setup

Open the app anytime at farmmanagerapp.netlify.app in your phone's browser.

  1. Install it to your home screen. On Android, tap the "Install app" button that appears at the top of the screen. On iPhone, tap the Share button, then "Add to Home Screen."
  2. Sign in (optional but recommended). Use your Google account or an email and password. This turns on free automatic backup — your data is safe even if you lose your phone.
  3. Set up your farm. Enter your farm's name, total area, and drop a pin on the map for its location — this is what your weather forecast is based on.
  4. Add your fields. One card per field or plot. You can trace each field's real shape on the satellite map for an exact area, instead of guessing.

The one-tap install button, and setting your farm's location

What's in the app

Overview

Your farm at a glance.

  • Total area, fields logged, fields at risk, spend this month
  • Live weather and a 5-day forecast for your farm's location
  • A work calendar for tasks and events, including rainfall
  • A chart showing soil moisture across every field
Map

Satellite view of every field.

  • Trace a field's real outline by hand for an exact area
  • Untraced fields just show as a pin until you measure them
Fields

One card per field — growth stage, moisture, fertilizer, pests, and more.

  • Set every field to the same growth stage at once, or one at a time
  • Moisture status updates itself using real rainfall when your last reading is getting old
Workers

A daily log of who worked which field, and when.

Expenses

Track costs by category — labor, fertilizer, fuel, and more — by field or for the whole farm.

Export

Download any of your data as a spreadsheet, or back up everything as one file.

Settings

Your farm's name, area, and location, plus whether recommendations are shown.

How to enter your data

Add a field

  1. Go to Fields and tap + Add field.
  2. Give it a name you already use on the farm, and its variety.
  3. Set its growth stage from the dropdown at the top of the card.
  4. Tap Save field.

Trace a field's real shape on the map

  1. Go to Map, find the field, and tap Measure on satellite map.
  2. Tap points around the field's real edges on the satellite image.
  3. Confirm — the exact area is calculated and filled in for you.

The Map tab — real traced field boundaries over satellite imagery

Move every field to the next stage at once

  1. On Fields, use the Set all fields' stage… dropdown next to "Add field."
  2. Pick the stage the whole farm is moving into.
  3. Confirm. Any field that's actually different can still be corrected on its own card right after.

The Fields tab — note "Adequate (19mm rain today)": moisture updating itself from real rainfall

Log fertilizer, irrigation, pests, and moisture

  1. Tap a field's card to open it.
  2. Fill in moisture, irrigation, pest status, leaf color, and the fertilizer mix used.
  3. Tap Save field — this feeds the farm-wide moisture chart on Overview too.

The full field edit form

Log a worker or an expense

  1. Go to Workers or Expenses.
  2. Enter the field, date, and amount (for expenses) or name (for workers).
  3. Save — it rolls into that field's totals and the farm summary automatically.

Logging an expense

Expenses roll up automatically into spending charts by category and by field

Log a task or an event on the calendar

  1. Go to Overview and find the Work calendar.
  2. Tap + Add a task for something to do (irrigation, weeding), or + Log an event for something that happened (heavy rain, a pest outbreak).
  3. Fill in the date and field, then save. If you pick a field, marking the task done logs a note on that field automatically.

Adding a task to the work calendar

Back up your data

  1. Go to Export and tap Download full backup.
  2. Save that file somewhere safe — do this regularly, and before switching phones.

The Export tab

Careful: restoring a backup replaces everything currently saved. Only use "Restore from backup" when you mean to overwrite what's there.
Sugarcane Farm Manager — Version 1.0 farmmanagerapp.netlify.app
